riskFactors: CPOOL is a protocol utility/governance token, not a direct claim on Clearpool loan pools, USDX reserves, T-bills, or receivable-backed vault assets.
Market data is volatile: CoinGecko snapshot shows small-cap token metrics with about $23.65M market cap and about $881K 24h volume on 2026-06-12.
The token is down substantially from its all-time high according to CoinGecko, indicating material market-price risk.
Protocol exposure is tied to Clearpool adoption, borrower quality, credit-market cycles, staking participation, and protocol revenue/fee sustainability.
No public legal opinion, securities-registration analysis, or regulator-supervised fund structure was normalized for CPOOL.
Multi-chain deployments increase bridge, monitoring, and contract-address verification complexity.
DeFiLlama reports protocol TVL is highly concentrated on Flare, so protocol activity concentration should be monitored.
mitigants: Clearpool has official documentation, official contract listings, and a dedicated contracts/audits page.
Clearpool docs describe audited on-chain pools and real-world lending products, including Dynamic, Prime, Fintech Vaults, RLOC, and USDX Treasury Pool.
CPOOL has exchange availability across multiple centralized venues and DEX venues listed in official Clearpool documentation.
Clearpool docs explicitly define CPOOL utility: delegated staking, borrower staking requirements, LP rewards, and governance participation.
DeFiLlama tracks Clearpool protocol TVL, fees, yields, active loans, liquidity, and chain distribution, improving market observability.
The asset is classified under governance_protocol, so missing reserve/custodian/redemption fields are not treated as asset-backed blockers.

_meta.notes: Risk score is a governance/protocol profile score, not comparable one-to-one with asset-backed institutional-grade scores.
